# Playwright Test Coverage: Federated Cloud Topology

## Objective
Establish rigorous test coverage strictly for the multi-tenant, `NEO_CHROMA_UNIFIED=false` configuration containing network boundaries and identity isolation.

## Tasks
1. Isolate test cases in Playwright that specifically focus on network isolation and Federated querying.
2. Verify that `DreamService` correctly instantiates a secondary REST client to query the Knowledge Base mock across the conceptual network.
3. Validate that simulated HTTP proxy headers correctly translate into ChromaDB `userId` metadata restrictions using mocked OIDC headers.

### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-05-04T20:45:01Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Disposition recommendation per #10691 / #10694: demote to non-default diagnostic
> 
> Posting a disposition note per [#10691](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10691) AC6 + [#10694](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10694) AC6 — the shared KB/MC team deployment MVP I'm documenting in [`learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md) (PR pending) explicitly demotes federated cloud topology from first-class product mode to **non-default diagnostic / future cloud-isolation track**.
> 
> **Rationale:**
> - The shared-team need driving the MVP (#10691) is **shared institutional memory**, which a federated topology fragments by default (each service owns its own Chroma).
> - Operating a federated topology is more complex (two Chroma services to manage) without serving the immediate MVP need.
> - The federated code paths (`chromaUnified=false`) remain functional and tested for the per-developer local default; demotion specifically targets the *cloud federated* deployment case.
> 
> **What this means for #10009:**
> - The ticket itself is **not a duplicate** of #10008 — different topology surfaces, different test contracts. Both deserve coverage.
> - The **priority** of #10009's test coverage relative to #10008's is now lower for the MVP. #10008 is the gated validation for the supported product path (shared cloud); #10009 is diagnostic-only coverage for a non-default mode.
> - I'm not auto-closing or relabeling — that's an operator/maintainer call. Three options for whoever picks this up:
>   1. **Keep open with a `non-mvp` or `diagnostic-only` label** — preserves the test-coverage commitment but signals priority.
>   2. **Close as superseded** with reference to the new SharedDeployment.md profile + #10008 as the active validation track.
>   3. **Retain at current priority** — counter-argument: federated topology may matter for downstream tenant-isolation work (#10011) and removing test coverage now creates regression risk.
> 
> My lean is **(1)**: keep open, add `non-mvp` if that label exists or surfaces it via a rename. The code path is real, the test coverage value is real, but it's not the MVP-critical path.
> 
> Cross-references:
> - [#10691](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10691) — Shared KB/MC Team Deployment MVP (parent)
> - [#10694](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10694) — this disposition is the deliverable for AC6
> - [#10008](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10008) — sibling test ticket for unified mode (the active MVP validation track)
> - `learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md` (PR pending) — Federated Mode Disposition section captures the rationale in long form
> 
> Origin Session ID: `7e52099b-9632-4c67-a2a1-4e1a1ad1c414`

* docs(agentos): document shared KB/MC team deployment profile (#10694)

Adds learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md as the canonical operator-facing profile
for the shared-team MVP topology under #10691. Indexes the new doc in
learn/tree.json under the AgentOS top-level surface, alongside KnowledgeBase /
MemoryCore / GitHubWorkflow / CodeExecution.

The doc establishes three independent boundaries (one Chroma process, separate
collections, two MCP servers), pins NEO_CHROMA_UNIFIED as the single
operator-facing flag, cross-links the existing healthcheck topology block from
MemoryCore.md (#10127), and documents migration from per-developer local mode.

#10008 is named as the canonical validation path (Playwright unified-mode test
coverage); #10009 receives an explicit demoted-to-non-default-diagnostic
disposition recommendation per epic-review structural amendments. No code paths
change — the unified topology and healthcheck shipped via #10001/#10007/#10127.

* docs(agentos): clarify per-server healthcheck shape + smoke-test surfaces (#10694)

Addresses Cycle 1 review (PRR_kwDODSospM77vMjD) on PR #10716. Two doc-contract
corrections in the Migration section:

- Step 4 (healthcheck verification): split per-server proof shape — Memory
  Core's healthcheck surfaces `database.topology` (canonical topology proof
  per #10127), Knowledge Base's healthcheck proves connectivity + collection
  availability without a topology block. Cross-server consistency is the
  end-to-end verification primitive.
- Step 5 (first-session smoke test): distinguish memory-visibility proof
  (`query_summaries` against Memory Core) from KB-sharing validation
  (`ask_knowledge_base` against Knowledge Base). The two surfaces are
  separate retrieval contracts; conflating them obscured what each query
  actually proves.

No content/scope changes — purely doc-precision corrections per @neo-gpt's
review.
